definition: OPEC+ fiscal breakeven oil prices constrain the global supply response. Aggregate weighted breakeven: $78-85/bbl (Saudi $85-96, UAE $60-65, Iraq $85-90, Russia $70-75). Historical pattern of fiscal stress → above-quota cheating → diplomatic friction → strategy shift documented across 4+ cycles.
evidence: IMF Article IV fiscal data for individual producers
Saudi breakeven risen from $65-70 (2019) to $85-96 (2025-26) due to Vision 2030
Sub-$75 oil historically precedes intra-OPEC discord and compliance breakdowns
open_questions: How much flexibility does Saudi Arabia have to defer Vision 2030 spending?
Would a credible slowdown lower the breakeven by $10-15/bbl?
How does Saudi reserve drawdown capacity (~$420B) extend the effective timeline?
